assumptions: The intragroup dividend has already been declared and recognised as an intragroup monetary receivable/payable.

The receivable/payable is denominated in a currency that creates foreign exchange exposure between group entities with different functional currencies, so the related exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
The general hedge accounting requirements in IFRS 9.6.4.1 are otherwise satisfied.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup dividend receivable/payable is an intragroup monetary item, and the context states that its foreign currency risk may qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements when the exchange differences affect consolidated profit or loss.
